recent_calls.summary: The user called and requested to speak to Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user to Amy and successfully transferred the call.
The user requested to speak to Amy, but did not engage further with the agent. The agent attempted to assist or pass a message but received no response, and the call ended due to inactivity.
The user called to inquire about their appointment but expressed frustration with the AI system. The agent attempted to assist but quickly transferred the call to a live agent for further help.

The user called after receiving a missed call and wanted to set up a schedule. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user reported an emergency water leak at a property in Saint Charles and requested immediate plumbing service. The agent confirmed the details and attempted to schedule an appointment, but the earliest available slots were for next week, which did not meet the user's urgent need. The call ended with the user expressing frustration and no appointment was booked for the same day.

The user called to inquire about used oil services and requested to speak with Mike. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent named Mike and successfully transferred the call. No further conversation occurred after the transfer.
The user requested to speak to Patrick, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate person. The transfer was successful and completed without issues.
The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

Alejandro from Hard Financing called to assist the user with account setup, answer questions, and schedule personalized onboarding training. The user did not respond during the call, and Alejandro requested a callback to schedule the training.
The agent confirmed the user's identity and phone number before transferring the call to a live agent for further assistance. The user inquired about their appointment scheduled for today.
The user requested to speak to a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called to inquire about billing. The agent informed the user that they do not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

Avery Rich from the Saint Louis Home Builders Association called to invite Budget Heating, Cooling and Plumbing to exhibit at their fall home show in Saint Charles from October 2nd to 4th. The agent confirmed the details and noted Avery's request for a callback for more information, promising to pass the message to the team. The call ended with a polite exchange.
The user initiated a call and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent greeted the user and then proceeded to transfer the call successfully.
The call was very brief and disconnected quickly, reaching voicemail without any meaningful interaction or task completion.

The user called to request assistance with filling out a lien waiver related to a property at 7529 Merillac Drive, Saint Louis, Missouri. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to the appropriate team for further help.

The user, Mignon, called to schedule a home warranty service appointment for their AC, which is not pushing air through the house. The agent confirmed the details and recorded the request for a technician to come out today, with a follow-up to confirm availability. The call ended with the user confirming the information was correct.

The user, Dorothy Harris, called to check the status of her seat repair. The agent confirmed her identity and phone number, found her appointment scheduled for June 17th with technician Amory, and then transferred her to a live representative upon her request.
The user requested to speak to an operator, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
The user called and requested to speak to an operator. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Suzette from American Home Shield called to provide dispatch details to Sophie at Budget. They exchanged information about the service address and the nature of the issue, but the call ended before all details were fully confirmed. The call was disconnected by the user after about 1 minute and 24 seconds.

The user called to request a paid invoice for air conditioner service. The agent informed the user that billing information was not accessible and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user requested to speak to someone, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
The user inquired about payment plans, and the agent informed them that billing information was not directly accessible but transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

The user, Sarah from Business Loan Funding, left a voicemail about a business line of credit opportunity and urged a callback before the review window closes. The call was not answered live and ended with the voicemail message.
The user called to speak with a representative about a scheduled appointment for tomorrow. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.

The user called to request a service appointment for a water heater with a pilot light that won't relight. The agent gathered the user's information and explained that the earliest available appointments are next Wednesday, June 24th. The user decided to try another service provider for an earlier appointment and declined further scheduling assistance.
The user called to speak with CG about rescheduling an appointment with Brian. The agent attempted to connect the user to the appropriate live agent and successfully transferred the call.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to another agent.

The user called to check if their AC unit was under warranty before scheduling a service appointment. The agent gathered necessary information and confirmed that someone from the team would contact the user with warranty details. The user declined information about maintenance programs and confirmed the summary provided by the agent.

The user, Nya, called to schedule a standard service appointment for their cooling unit, which is running but not blowing air. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for June 23rd at 2 PM. The user declined to provide feedback and the call ended successfully.

The user, identifying as American Home Shield, called to get a status update on a work order. After some clarification on the user's identity, the agent transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user called to reschedule a cooling service appointment due to the system freezing up. The agent provided available slots near the user's preferred time, but the user ultimately decided to keep the original appointment. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that a human agent would follow up to finalize the appointment.

The user called to inquire about recharging their mini split system and provided their contact and service details. The agent informed the user that the earliest available service appointments are on June 24th, 25th, or 26th at 4 PM, and the user decided to call back later to confirm a suitable time. The call ended with the user satisfied and no immediate booking made.

Walter called to report that his air conditioning is not working and the air compressor outside isn't starting, which he considers an emergency. The agent documented the issue, confirmed the service address and contact details, and flagged the request as a priority for follow-up on the next business day. Walter requested service as soon as possible today but was informed that a technician could not be dispatched immediately due to after-hours, and the call ended with the agent assuring a prompt follow-up.

The user called Budget's after-hours service to inquire about the time window for a service appointment. The agent informed the user that specific timing could not be confirmed before regular business hours but noted the user's preference for a morning appointment and assured a follow-up on the next business day.

The user called to inquire about the scheduled arrival time of a technician. The agent informed the user that the time could not be confirmed before business hours but assured that the team would reach out after 8 AM to provide details. The user did not request any further assistance.

The user, Terrie, called to leave a voicemail requesting to reschedule a cooling appointment to Monday because she still does not have a seal. The agent confirmed the details and informed Terrie that the team will follow up the next business day. Terrie declined information about maintenance programs and expressed dissatisfaction with the virtual assistant.

Mignon Sanchez called to report that her AC is running but not pushing air, describing it as a comfort issue needing urgent attention. The agent confirmed her service address and contact details, marked the request as urgent, and informed her that a representative would follow up on the next business day. Mignon declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests before ending the call.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing themselves and no further interaction from the user before the call ended.

Tamia from American Home Shield called Budget's after-hours assistant, Evie, to request a report for a specific work order number. Evie confirmed the work order number and assured Tamia that the request would be passed along for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended politely with no further requests.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user, Pat, called to inquire about service costs for checking Freon and inspecting a small water leak. The agent collected service details, confirmed contact information, and noted Pat's preferred appointment times. Pat expressed possible interest in maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.

Linda called to inquire about a specific question regarding the technician scheduled to come on Friday. The agent informed her that it was after-hours and she could not connect her to a live person but documented her question. Linda decided to call back the next day.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing herself and asking how she could help, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The call was initiated by the user but was very brief and disconnected abruptly without completing the conversation or addressing any service needs.
